skill.name: animate
skill.description: Review a feature and enhance it with purposeful animations, micro-interactions, and motion effects that improve usability and delight.
skill.summary: Improve frontend features with purposeful animation, motion feedback, and accessibility-aware interaction design.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown prose and fenced examples. The skill contains guidance for UI animation only, with no executable scripts, network access, credential access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings are Markdown inline code or fenced CSS and JavaScript examples. No Ruby backtick execution or executable shell command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible Markdown formatting and example CSS or JavaScript guidance. There is no command interpreter, script file, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ordinary Prose Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections point to natural language about features, targets, review, and loading states. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing API is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no encryption, hashing, or security-sensitive code at these lines. The matches appear to come from short substrings inside normal documentation text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Performance Guidance Misclassified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detection appears on a line advising GPU-friendly animation properties. It does not collect system information or inspect the host environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line gives frontend performance advice about transform and opacity. No commands, APIs, or instructions gather machine, network, or user data.

content.value_statement: Static interfaces can feel abrupt, unclear, or unresponsive. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code add focused animation that improves feedback, flow, and accessibility.
actual_capabilities: Reviews a target feature for useful animation opportunities.
Plans entrance, feedback, transition, guidance, and delight motion layers.
Recommends practical timing, easing, and interaction patterns.
Prioritizes transform and opacity for smoother frontend performance.
Guides verification of smoothness, timing, and interaction behavior.
limitations: Requires project context before implementation decisions are reliable.
Does not include executable animation libraries or helper scripts.
Depends on the host model to edit application code correctly.
May need adaptation for strict brand systems or motion-sensitive audiences.

use_cases.title: Improve Product Flows
use_cases.description: Add subtle feedback and state transitions to forms, navigation, and confirmation moments.

use_cases.title: Polish Frontend Components
use_cases.description: Enhance buttons, modals, tabs, drawers, and loading states with clear motion behavior.

use_cases.title: Prepare Accessible Motion Reviews
use_cases.description: Check that animation choices include reduced-motion handling and do not block interaction.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review for a focused UI element.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the animate skill to review this component and suggest purposeful micro-interactions. Focus on feedback, timing, and reduced-motion support.

prompt_templates.title: Add Motion to a Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate implementation for an existing feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the animate skill on this feature. Identify the main animation opportunities, then implement the highest-value motion changes without making the interface feel busy.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Motion System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across a product area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the animate skill to create a motion strategy for this product area. Define entrance, feedback, transition, and accessibility rules before editing code.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Motion Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the animate skill to audit current animations. Check performance, timing, interaction blocking, reduced-motion behavior, and consistency with the product tone.

output_examples.input: A settings form has instant validation messages and no button feedback.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies missing feedback, proposes fast button press states, smooth validation transitions, and reduced-motion-safe alternatives.

output_examples.input: A dashboard loads cards and charts at the same time.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a short staggered reveal, restrained chart entrance motion, and performance checks for lower-powered devices.

output_examples.input: A modal opens abruptly and traps attention poorly.
output_examples.output: The assistant adds a brief backdrop fade, modal entrance transition, focus handling checks, and a faster exit animation.

best_practices: Gather audience, brand tone, use case, and performance constraints before adding motion.
Use one clear signature animation and reserve smaller motion for feedback and transitions.
Test reduced-motion behavior, frame rate, timing, and interaction continuity.
anti_patterns: Adding animation everywhere without a user experience reason.
Using long feedback animations that make controls feel slow.
Animating layout properties when transform or opacity can achieve the effect.

faq.question: What does this skill help animate?
faq.answer: It helps improve frontend features, components, state changes, navigation, loading states, and small interaction feedback.

faq.question: Does it require a specific framework?
faq.answer: No. It mentions CSS, Web Animations API, Framer Motion, and GSAP as options, but it does not require one.

faq.question: Does it consider accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires support for prefers-reduced-motion and asks for alternatives for users who need less motion.

faq.question: Can it write production code?
faq.answer: The skill provides implementation guidance. Actual code quality depends on the host assistant and the project context.

faq.question: Is this useful for serious business software?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports restrained motion for clarity, feedback, and smoother state changes without requiring playful effects.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the target feature, audience, brand tone, performance limits, and any accessibility or design system requirements.
